svg.title: Private IP address allocation in AWS VPC versus Synology Docker
svg.desc: Diagram showing the three RFC1918 private IP ranges and how AWS uses 10.0.0.0/16 for VPCs while Synology Docker uses 172.17.0.0/16 by default for container networks.
svg.text: Three ranges reserved for any private network
How each platform carves its range into subnets

g.text: EC2 gets next free IP in subnet
Container gets next IP from pool

svg.text: Both follow RFC 1918. AWS picks 10.x because it scales; Docker picks 172.17 to stay out of your home 192.168 range.
If your home LAN ever uses 172.17.x, change the Docker default in /etc/docker/daemon.json before they collide.
